source_jobs.title: Principal Product Manager
source_jobs.description:
About the role:

We’re looking for a Principal Product Manager to define the future of our Enterprise Customer Education segment, helping Absorb’s enterprise clients train and enable their customers at scale.

This is a high-impact individual contributor role that blends strategy, creativity, and analytical rigor. You’ll uncover new opportunities, validate ideas through data and prototypes, and partner with cross-functional leaders to turn promising concepts into outcomes that move the business forward.

You’ll thrive if you’re part strategist, part innovator, and part storyteller - comfortable connecting dots across data, customer insight, and emerging technology (especially AI) to craft bold yet pragmatic bets that shape Absorb's future.







What you’ll do:

 - Own and evangelize the segment strategy: Build a deep understanding of the customer education market, customer needs, and where we can create meaningful differentiation.

 - Uncover and size opportunities: Use data, research, and customer insight to identify where to play and how to win.

 - Validate early ideas: Partner closely with design and engineering to prototype, test, and refine new concepts - fast.

 - Build alignment and conviction: Create clear, data-backed business cases that bring senior leaders and teams along.

 - Guide teams toward impact: Stay involved as ideas move into execution - clarifying outcomes, connecting dots, and ensuring what we build delivers on the intent. Represent and communicate progress towards key goals and metrics.

 - Lead through influence: Provide dotted-line leadership to PMs across the segment - offering strategic guidance, coaching, and input into their performance to ensure alignment and impact.

 - Bring an AI lens: Explore how new technologies can help customers learn more effectively and scale education across enterprises.

 - Partner and Educate: Act as a thought leader and advocate for customer education within the organization.

What you’ll bring:

 - 7+ years of Product Management experience in B2B SaaS or enterprise software, with a track record of shaping strategy and driving measurable business outcomes.

 - Proven ability to translate insights into strategy - combining data, customer research, and business acumen to identify and prioritize high-impact opportunities.

 - Experience in early-stage or 0-1 product environments, with comfort navigating ambiguity and rapid iteration.

 - Strong analytical and financial modeling skills, capable of building data-backed business cases and forecasts.

 - Skilled at communicating with clarity and influence, from executive presentations to cross-functional collaboration.

 - Comfortable working across design, engineering, and GTM teams to validate and evolve ideas into actionable plans.

 - Curiosity and fluency in AI and emerging technologies, and how they can reshape customer education and enterprise learning.

 - (Bonus) Experience in customer education, enablement, or learning technology domains.

Are you ready to become an Absorber?

What we offer:

 - Fully remote-first work with flexible work arrangements 🏡

 - Comprehensive Health and Wellness Benefits including retirement savings programs, eligibility for two different bonus plans, generous time off, comprehensive medical and dental benefits based on your country of location 🧘

 - New Hire Equipment Allowance and monthly Flex Allowance to support your success 📦

 - Endless opportunity for career growth and internal mobility 🌱

 - Employee driven DE&I programs 🫶

 

Who are we?

Absorb Software is the leading global AI-driven learning platform provider, helping organizations unlock the full potential of their workforce. source_jobs.title: Senior Web Design Engineer
source_jobs.description:
"The Marketing team is poised for unprecedented growth and opportunity. We're in a very unique position where we've experienced tremendous success but are far from exhausting the whitespace around us. There is a unique opportunity to join a rising organization and make a significant and immediate impact." Mark Lynch - Senior Director, Demand Generation

The Web Design Engineer leads the construction of our marketing website, blending visual design and front-end engineering to create production-ready web experiences that are semantically correct, accessible, performant, and visually refined. This role collaborates with designers, content strategists, demand generation, and our developer to elevate everything we release on absorblms.com http://absorblms.com, oversees implementation of components and pages in our Webflow-based stack, and shapes the architecture and reusable patterns for the team.

 

Unlike traditional IDE-only front-end roles, most production work is done in Webflow's visual development environment, requiring strong skills in HTML, CSS, DOM structure, responsive design, accessibility, browser performance, and client-side behavior. The ideal candidate is a browser-native web design engineer who cares about the quality of what ships.



What you’ll do:

 - Implement marketing website components, layouts, and pages to a high standard of HTML semantics, CSS quality, accessibility, performance, and visual polish.

 - Translate Figma designs into production web output, partnering with designers to refine responsive behavior, spacing, type, hierarchy, motion, and interaction details before and during implementation.

 - Own the quality bar for production web output, including reviewing work produced by other team members and helping establish standards, patterns, and reusable components.

 - Build custom interactive components – including Web Components and vanilla JavaScript modules – where the intersection of design craft and engineering is required.

 - Implement simple motion and animation as part of component and page work (e.g., CSS transitions, menu fade-ins, hover and focus states, lightweight interactions).

 - Contribute to our Next.js applications and shared component library (via Webflow DevLink) to keep design and behavior consistent across the marketing site and custom web apps.

 - Diagnose and resolve front-end issues using browser DevTools, including layout, rendering, performance, accessibility, and cross-browser problems.

 - Optimize Core Web Vitals and page load performance, including responsive image strategies, resource hints, render-blocking mitigation, script loading, and minimizing layout shift.

 - Implement accessibility best practices, including semantic HTML, heading structure, focus management, keyboard interaction, reduced-motion considerations, and appropriate ARIA usage.

 - Implement and QA A/B tests, personalization experiences, and conversion optimization experiments in partnership with marketing stakeholders.

 - Help reduce fragile one-off implementations by creating durable, reusable patterns and clear standards across components, layouts, and interactions.

 - Partner with the Content and Demand Gen teams as a technical resource for advanced layout, component, and interaction questions.

 - Contribute to the architecture and reusable patterns that underpin the marketing stack, including component design, CSS variable systems, and interaction patterns.

 - Make pragmatic trade-off decisions that balance design intent, engineering quality, timeline, and business impact.

What you’ll bring:

 - 8+ years of professional experience building production web interfaces at the intersection of design and front-end engineering.

 - Deep proficiency in semantic HTML and modern CSS, including grid, flexbox, custom properties, container queries, logical properties, and the full selector vocabulary.

 - Strong visual eye, with the ability to translate Figma designs faithfully and to identify a

source_jobs.title: Performance Marketing Manager
source_jobs.description:
"The Marketing team is poised for unprecedented growth and opportunity. We're in a very unique position where we've experienced tremendous success but are far from exhausting the whitespace around us. There is a unique opportunity to join a rising organization and make a significant and immediate impact." Mark Lynch - Senior Director, Demand Generation.

Are you a data-driven marketer with a passion for crafting high-impact paid search campaigns? Absorb is looking for a strategic Performance Marketing Manager to help fuel our growth. In this role, you’ll take the lead on developing and executing best-in-class search strategies across multiple platforms, driving measurable results and continuous optimization. Working closely with a talented, collaborative team, you’ll play a key role in shaping our digital strategy and achieving ambitious growth goals. If you love blending analytical insight with creative PPC execution — and thrive in a fast-paced, results-driven environment — we’d love to connect.

Are you interested in hearing more about Absorb from a current employee? Listen here:



What you’ll do:

 - Architect & Execute Paid Search Strategies: Design and implement sophisticated paid search strategies that align with our offerings and support overall marketing and revenue objectives.

 - Campaign Management & Optimization: Drive daily management of search campaigns across Google Ads, Microsoft Advertising, and emerging platforms, leveraging advanced testing methodologies and analytics to maximize ROI and quality lead generation.

 - Performance Analysis & Optimization: Translate complex data into actionable insights using advanced analytics tools to continually refine keywords, ad copy, bidding strategies, and landing page performance.

 - Cross-Functional Leadership: Partner with content, creative, and product teams to ensure cohesive messaging and seamless customer journeys from search to conversion, amplifying brand impact.

 - Budget Stewardship: Strategically allocate and optimize search marketing investments across channels, identifying opportunities to scale high-performing campaigns while maintaining efficiency.

 - Executive Reporting & Strategy: Deliver concise, insight-driven performance reports to leadership, highlighting growth opportunities and providing data-backed recommendations for strategic decisions.

 - Innovation & Competitive Edge: Stay at the forefront of search marketing trends, emerging technologies, and competitive strategies to continuously evolve our approach in the dynamic LMS landscape.

What you’ll bring:

 - Proven Search Marketing Expertise: 10+ years of hands-on experience managing sophisticated paid search campaigns, with demonstrated success in B2B SaaS or technology sectors.

 - Data-Driven Decision Making: Exceptional analytical skills and fluency with Google Analytics, Google Ads, Microsoft Advertising, and performance tracking tools to inform strategic decisions.

 - Strategic Vision & Tactical Excellence: Ability to develop comprehensive search strategies while executing precise tactical initiatives that deliver measurable business impact.

 - Technical Proficiency: Advanced knowledge of automation, A/B testing methodologies, and sophisticated bidding strategies. Experience with marketing automation platforms and CRM integration is highly valued.

 - Collaborative Leadership: Outstanding communication and influence skills to effectively partner across departments, translating complex search insights into compelling business strategies.

 - Adaptive Problem Solving: Proactive mindset with demonstrated ability to identify challenges, develop innovative solutions, and thrive in a fast-paced environment.

 - Growth Mindset: Passionate about continuous improvement, with a relentless focus on optimizing performance metrics and staying ahead of industry developments.

Are you ready to become an Absorber?

What we offer:

 - Fully remote-first work with flexible work arrangements

source_jobs.title: Customer Advocacy and Engagement Manager
source_jobs.description:
"Every customer has a story worth telling and a lesson worth learning. When we amplify those voices, we don't just inspire others; we unlock the potential for every learner to achieve something extraordinary. As our Engagement & Advocacy Manager, you'll be the architect of those stories, turning customer success into a movement that drives real impact. This role sits at the heart of our business and our community, championing the voices that help other customers invest in learning, lead with confidence, and build cultures that last."- Leslie Kelley, Chief Growth officer



We’re looking for an Customer Advocacy and Engagement Manager to build and scale programs that deepen customer engagement and generate authentic customer voice.

In this role, you will create meaningful experiences through community, events, and recognition programs that drive participation and turn engaged customers into advocates. You’ll also own scalable advocacy initiatives, including review generation and testimonial programs, helping amplify the voice of our customers in the market.

This is a highly cross-functional role that partners closely with Customer Success, Marketing, and Enablement to connect engagement with adoption, retention, and growth.





What you’ll do:

 - Design and scale customer engagement programs, including community initiatives, webinars, events, and recognition programs

 - Grow and activate our customer community, increasing participation and peer-to-peer engagement

 - Build and manage scalable advocacy programs, including review generation (e.g., G2), testimonials, and customer stories

 - Identify and nurture engaged customers into advocates and contributors

 - Partner with Customer Success and Implementation teams to align advocacy efforts and ensure a seamless customer experience

 - Collaborate with Lifecycle Marketing to promote programs and increase participation

 - Track and optimize performance based on engagement, participation, and advocacy metrics



What Success Looks like:

 - Increased customer participation in community and programs

 - A thriving, engaged customer community

 - Consistent generation of customer reviews and advocacy

 - Strong pipeline of engaged customers ready to participate in case studies and other advocacy initiatives



What you’ll bring:

 - 4–8 years of experience in customer marketing, community, advocacy, or engagement roles in B2B SaaS

 - Proven ability to build and scale customer programs or communities

 - Experience driving customer advocacy initiatives, including review programs or testimonials

 - Strong program management and cross-functional collaboration skills

 - Data-driven mindset with the ability to measure and improve performance

 - Excellent communication and relationship-building skills

source_jobs.title: Business Development Representative
source_jobs.description:
Absorb, a SaaS company, is achieving remarkable 45% year-over-year growth without relying on Outbound Selling. Since joining just a month ago, I've been amazed by the talented Sales team's expertise in the LMS and eLearning space, driving our growth through Digital Marketing. But there's untapped potential. We're forming a dedicated outbound team of rockstars to hunt new customer segments beyond traditional channels. With my track record in building strong Account-Based Outbound teams, we aim to unlock Absorb's enormous potential. Join us in making Absorb history!" – Kim Brown, VP, Business Development


As a Business Development Representative (BDR) you will play a pivotal role in driving our growth by identifying and qualifying outbound opportunities. Leveraging your self-initiating resilience, work ethic, and self-awareness you will engage with prospects, create interest, and showcase the advantages of our Learning Management Solution. Your resilient and persistent approach to cold calling, coupled with effective time management and prioritization, will contribute to exceeding sales quotas and driving new bookings within assigned territories.

Join us in shaping the future of learning solutions and be a key player in Absorb LMS's success!



What you’ll do:

 - Source new leads through innovative outbound methods, demonstrating creativity in prospecting.

 - Create and/or identify high-quality opportunities by discovering and qualifying outbound leads.

 - Effectively communicate the Absorb value proposition to new prospects.

 - Consistently achieve daily/weekly/monthly goals for calls, emails, prospecting, with a proactive outreach approach.

 - Attain weekly, monthly, and quarterly pipeline goals, qualifying leads to appropriate criteria for overall sales success.

 - Develop and execute weekly call plans and quarterly account plans, collaborating with the account team for strategic success.

 - Identify and engage with sales opportunities, demonstrating a strong understanding of various industry issues and the role of an LMS.

 - Maintain CRM tools with up-to-date client information and actively participate in team meetings and projects to improve processes and drive sales output.

What you’ll bring:

 - 1-3 years experience in a related role.

 - Must be a team player and highly self-motivated.

 - Excellent at being adaptable to change and switching priorities.

 - The ability to deal with and thrive on objections and rejections daily.

 - Proficient in conducting detailed data analyses and using metrics to drive business results.

 - Very strong verbal and written communication skills.

 - Interested in long-term career opportunities in SaaS sales.

Additional preferred qualifications:

 - Proven ability to sell with a minimum of 1 year of experience. This can be in the service industry, field sales, door to door, retail or at an office

 - Familiarity with Sales Engagement tools (Outreach, SalesLoft, Salesforce)

 - Familiarity with Sales Prospecting tools (ZoomInfo, Lusha)

 - Understanding of sales process and qualifying such as MEDDPICC, BANT, SPIN, Challenger

Technologies we use:

 - Absorb LMS, Salesforce, Salesloft, ZoomInfo, Gong, Teams, etc..

source_jobs.title: Senior Manager, Marketing AI & Analytics
source_jobs.description:
"The Marketing team is poised for unprecedented growth and opportunity. We're in a very unique position where we've experienced tremendous success but are far from exhausting the whitespace around us. There is a unique opportunity to join a rising organization and make a significant and immediate impact." Mark Lynch - Senior Director, Demand Generation

This is not a traditional marketing operations role. We're looking for someone who believes the highest-leverage thing a marketing team can do right now is build with AI — not just adopt AI tools, but architect workflows, deploy solutions, and fundamentally rethink how a lean team competes with organizations five times its size.

You'll inherit an established data infrastructure and a marketing organization that's already running AI-powered pipelines in production — things like automated content generation, LLM-based lead scoring, customer risk monitoring, and intelligent reporting. The stack today includes BigQuery, Salesforce, Marketo, Power Automate, and Google Cloud, and the building happens in tools like Claude Code. Your job is to take ownership of that foundation, extend it, and use it to keep accelerating what this team can do.

You'll lead two junior analysts, replace external consultant dependencies, and work directly with the VP of Marketing as a strategic and technical partner. The thesis behind this role is simple: the right person with the right tools and data infrastructure can deliver more value than a team twice the size operating the old way. If that excites you, keep reading.



What you’ll do:

AI-Native Strategy & Execution

 - Identify high-impact opportunities to apply AI across marketing and build production-ready solutions (not concepts or decks)

 - Own end-to-end delivery: ideation, prototyping, validation, and deployment

 - Develop and maintain AI-driven workflows (e.g., content generation, lead scoring, competitive intel, reporting)

 - Evaluate emerging AI tools and lead build vs. buy decisions

 - Establish scalable frameworks, documentation, and adoption best practices

 - Act as the internal expert on applied AI, influencing strategy and upskilling teams

Marketing Data & Analytics Infrastructure

 - Own and evolve the marketing data stack (warehouse + integrations across CRM, automation, ads, and web analytics)

 - Build automated reporting that delivers actionable insights to stakeholders

 - Lead deep-dive analyses on pipeline, channel performance, attribution, lead quality, and ROI

 - Improve data quality, measurement frameworks, and reporting coverage

 - Manage data orchestration (APIs, workflows, automation pipelines)

Team Leadership & Operational Scale

 - Lead and develop a high-performing team of two analysts

 - Transition key work from external consultants to in-house capability

 - Partner closely with Marketing leadership to drive strategic initiatives

 - Deliver clear insights, recommendations, and materials to support executive decision-making

What you’ll bring:

 - You've built working AI solutions end-to-end — from identifying a problem, to prototyping with LLM APIs or agentic coding tools, to putting something in production that people actually use

 - You're comfortable with code, SQL, and data infrastructure — not necessarily as a trained engineer, but as someone who can ship working solutions that involve all three

 - Strong structured problem-solving — the kind honed in consulting, strategy roles, or high-accountability operating environments

 - 5–10 years of experience across some combination of: strategy, consulting, marketing operations, analytics, business operations, or revenue operations

 - Analytical depth: you can build a report, question the data, find the story, and present it clearly to a senior stakeholder

 - People leadership experience or strong evidence of mentoring and developing junior talent

 - High agency, low ego, and a bias toward building over recommending

source_jobs.title: Strategic Account Manager
source_jobs.description:
“At Absorb, we cultivate a culture of curiosity and continuous growth, both for ourselves and for our customers. We’re passionate about unlocking potential, challenging the status quo, and building solutions that empower our clients to achieve their boldest goals. Here, every day is an opportunity to innovate, learn, and make a meaningful impact. If you’re a Strategic Account Manager eager to make a real difference and empower organizations to be at the cutting edge of learning, this is your opportunity to build something truly special." James Levine, VP, Strategic Account Management

 

The Strategic Account Manager is a critical role at Absorb. We’re looking for someone who has demonstrated experience working with large, complex organizations having built strategic account plans and has gone on to successfully close meaningful land and expand opportunities with those accounts. Your past experiences, advice, and your sales acumen will have a direct impact on Absorb’s mission.

We’re looking for someone to step into the role with deep experience who can build relationships quickly and identify where opportunities exist in our current customer base. From there, you’ll need to communicate internally what you need to help you execute on those plans to see significant expansion within our most strategic customers. This role will be supported by all levels and sectors of Absorb. You’ll be able to interface with teams and executives across all business units at Absorb to help you, and our customers, succeed. If this sounds familiar and exciting to you, then we’re thrilled to have you join Absorb and make a big impact!



What you’ll do:

 - Build trust and strong business relationships with key decision makers in Absorb’s most opportune accounts

 - Generate strategic account plans to execute strategies which will lead to upsell/cross sell opportunities

 - Work with internal resources (Leadership, Solutions Engineers, Account Development reps) to develop and execute expansion plans from Discovery to Closed Won

 - Monitor your pipeline to ensure appropriate actions are taken at appropriate times to continue to facilitate the sale to closed won

 - Work collaboratively with the support and technology teams to become an expert in Absorb LMS and the overall learning management landscape.

 - Demonstrate Absorb LMS functionality and provide pricing options that align to customer objectives and assist Absorb’s continued growth

 - Work with existing Customer Success resources to identify risk and mitigate churn wherever possible

 - Use customer and prospect contact activities tools and systems, and update relevant information held in these systems

 - Monitor and report on market and competitor activities and provide relevant reports and information

What you’ll bring:

 - Strong background in technology sales and client management with a minimum 7+ years of experience

 - Ability to become a trusted advisor to senior leaders within client organizations

 - Very strong business communication skills (written and verbal)

 - Experience working with a global client base

 - Ability to travel for business when required

 - Strong internal team player

Additional preferred qualifications:

 - Post-secondary degree – business undergraduate degree or MBA

Technologies we use:

 - AbsorbLMS, Salesforce, Gainsight, Zendesk, Calendly, Zoom, Teams, Adobe Acrobat

source_jobs.title: VP, Enterprise Sales
source_jobs.description:
“Absorb Software is at an exciting point in its enterprise growth journey, and this VP, Enterprise Sales role offers a unique opportunity to build on a strong foundation across North America and EMEA. This leader will play a critical role in elevating performance, refining our go-to-market strategy, and driving high-quality revenue growth across complex SaaS deals. It’s an ideal role for someone who thrives on leading high-performing teams and making a lasting impact. – Mark Behl, CRO



About the role:

This is a high-impact, high-visibility leadership role where you’ll shape the future of our enterprise revenue strategy, owning the vision, execution, and results of a multi-region enterprise sales organization driving new logo growth across complex, high-value SaaS deals.

You’ll lead an established team of leaders and Account Executives across North America and EMEA, leading both inbound and outbound motions as part of a cohesive, high-performing engine. This is an opportunity to scale what’s working, elevate performance, and introduce what’s next in driving meaningful impact on deal execution and enterprise customer engagement.

If you’re energized by building winning teams, influencing strategy at the executive level, and leading in a fast-growing SaaS environment, this role offers the platform to truly make your mark.



What you’ll do:

Revenue Leadership & Execution

 - Own and deliver the enterprise bookings target, ensuring predictable, high-quality revenue growth

 - Lead and optimize a team responsible for $50K–$500K deal sizes, with a focus on scaling mid-to-top-end deal performance

 - Drive pipeline health, conversion improvements, and deal velocity across inbound and outbound motions

 - Establish forecasting discipline, ensuring accuracy and transparency across regions and teams

Team Leadership & Development

 - Lead and coach 4 Director-level leaders overseeing 20+ Account Executives

 - Develop leadership bench strength and succession plans across all levels

 - Build a high-performance, accountable, and metrics-driven sales culture

 - Implement and operationalize formal coaching structure and expectations

Go-To-Market Strategy

 - Refine and execute enterprise GTM strategy across NAM and EMEA markets

 - Partner cross-functionally with Product to influence product roadmap and ensure new logo enterprise perspective is strongly and accurately represented in product roadmap

 - Optimize inbound and outbound motions, ensuring clear segmentation, territory design, and coverage models

Operational Excellence

 - Drive data-informed decision-making across performance management, compensation design, and resource allocation

 - Improve sales processes, coaching strategies and cadence, and AE skills to increase win rates

Enterprise Deal Strategy

 - Personally engage in strategic, high-value deals to accelerate closures and mitigate risk

 - Elevate deal qualification, executive alignment, and value-based selling across the organization

 - Build executive relationships with key customers and prospects



What you’ll bring:

 - 7+ years of experience in SaaS sales, with a strong focus on enterprise segments

 - 2+ years of 2nd-line leadership experience (leading leaders) in a B2B SaaS environment

 - Demonstrated success managing $10M+ annual quotas and delivering consistent over-performance

 - Experience leading geographically distributed teams (North America + EMEA preferred)

 - Proven ability to manage both inbound and outbound enterprise sales motions

 - Track record closing and coaching teams on $50K–$500K+ deal sizes



Additional preferred qualifications:

 - Post-secondary degree – business undergraduate degree or MBA



Technologies we use:

AbsorbLMS, Salesforce, Gainsight, Zendesk, Calendly, Zoom, Teams, Adobe Acrobat

We’re hiring a Manager of Small & Mid-Market Sales (EMEA) to lead our team into a high-growth, largely untapped segment. This is a rare opportunity to shape strategy, unlock new revenue, and sell a truly best-in-class, cutting-edge LMS.

You’ll play a key role in scaling a high-performing team to meet surging demand across the region. Drawing on your proven success to coach and develop talented sales professionals, helping them elevate performance and exceed targets.

With a sharp eye for market trends and a strong ability to build pipeline, you’ll lead your team to drive meaningful growth and set a new benchmark for success at Absorb.



What you'll do:

 - Lead and inspire a team of quota-carrying Account Executives, to manage full sales cycles, build and maintain robust pipelines, and deliver accurate forecasting.

 - Provide strategic support for escalations and participate directly in complex negotiations and deal structuring.

 - Drive strategic growth by building and strengthening the company’s brand presence within the EMEA region, ensuring alignment with global objectives.

 - Hire, motivate, coach, and develop team members to maximize productivity and career growth.

 - Deliver detailed activity reports, opportunity management updates, quotes, and forecasts, along with verbal feedback to senior sales leadership.

 - Collaborate closely with Marketing, Customer Success, Product Management, and other cross-functional teams to drive alignment and success.

 - Inspire your team through vision, energy, and a commitment to excellence.

 - Meet or exceed team revenue, conversion, and enterprise growth targets.

 - Foster a collaborative, high-performance culture across all sales segments.

source_jobs.title: Vice President of Go-to-Market
source_jobs.description:
The Role

You will lead the team that converts pipeline into customers and expands revenue within the existing customer base. This is not a marketing-only or sales-only role — it sits at the intersection of product, sales, and customer success, owning how Absorb shows up in every buying and expansion conversation.

You will own product marketing, product evangelism, solution engineering, and analyst relations. Your team is the bridge between what we build and what customers buy. You are equally accountable for new-logo conversion and for positioning new products and capabilities to drive expansion within the installed base.

This role reports to the CMO & CPO and is part of the Product to Revenue leadership team — a unified organization spanning product, marketing, and go-to-market.



Why This Role Matters

Absorb is at an inflection point. We’re launching Aura (our AI learning agent), expanding into new product lines, and competing against legacy incumbents who are slower to innovate. The VP of GTM will be the person who translates that product advantage into revenue — both from new customers and from the thousands of existing customers who haven’t yet seen what’s possible.

Four of five functions in our Product to Revenue organization directly own GRR and NRR outcomes. This role is central to that mission.



What you'll do:

 - Positioning: Product Marketing & Messaging —

Own Absorb’s market positioning, competitive messaging, and product narratives for both prospects and existing customers. Ensure every sales conversation, product launch, and customer touchpoint tells a compelling, consistent story.

 - Proof of Value: Solution Engineering —

Lead a 21-person SE team that delivers demos, POCs, and technical validation. Drive win rates by making the buying experience as compelling as the product itself. Extend SE capabilities to expansion motions for existing customers evaluating new products.

 - Market Presence: Product Evangelism —

Build Absorb’s presence in the enterprise learning market through thought leadership, speaking, and community engagement. Position Absorb as the leader in AI-powered learning.

 - Market Credibility: Analyst Relations —

Own relationships with Forrester, Gartner, Brandon Hall, and other analysts. Drive placements and recognition that shorten sales cycles and validate our AI leadership position.

 - NRR: Expansion Positioning —

Partner with Customer Enablement and Sales to position new products, features, and use cases to existing customers. Create the materials, narratives, and proof points that drive upsell and cross-sell conversations.

Key Metrics You’ll Be Measured On

 - New-logo win rate improvement

 - Sales cycle length reduction

 - Expansion revenue influenced (cross-sell and upsell pipeline)

 - Competitive win rate in head-to-head evaluations

 - Analyst recognition and placement improvements

 - NRR contribution from GTM-sourced expansion opportunities

What you'll bring:

 - 10+ years in B2B SaaS, with at least 5 years leading product marketing, GTM, or revenue-facing teams at scale

 - Demonstrated track record of improving win rates and shortening sales cycles through positioning, enablement, and proof-of-value programs

 - Experience owning both new-logo conversion AND expansion/retention outcomes — not just top-of-funnel

 - Deep comfort with solution engineering or pre-sales teams; you understand the demo-to-close motion

 - Experience in enterprise software (LMS, HCM, EdTech, or adjacent markets preferred but not required)

Jason R - Manager, Software Development

About the Role:

As a Senior DevOps Engineer at Absorb, you’ll play a key role in optimizing the reliability, scalability, and performance of our cloud-based (SaaS) Learning Management System.

You’ll partner closely with product development and operations teams to ensure our platform delivers a rich feature set, high availability, and a seamless experience for our customers.

 - 4+ years in DevOps, SRE, or cloud engineering roles

 - Strong experience with AWS (Solutions Architect certification is a plus)

 - Hands-on experience with:

 - Infrastructure as Code (AWS CDK, CloudFormation)

 - CI/CD systems and deployment pipelines

 - Monitoring, observability, and performance tuning

 - Scripting experience (PowerShell preferred)

 - Experience supporting and optimizing web applications at scale

 - Strong problem-solving skills with the ability to balance urgency and thoughtful execution

 - Ability to work cross-functionally across teams and time zones

Preferred experience

 - Containerization (Docker, ECS, Kubernetes)

 - CI/CD tooling (Git Lab, Bamboo or similar)

 - Familiarity with .NET / C# ecosystem

source_jobs.description:
"The Marketing team is poised for unprecedented growth and opportunity. We're in a very unique position where we've experienced tremendous success but are far from exhausting the whitespace around us. There is a unique opportunity to join a rising organization and make a significant and immediate impact." Mark Lynch - Senior Director, Demand Generation

The Web Design Engineer leads the construction of our marketing website, blending visual design and front-end engineering to create production-ready web experiences that are semantically correct, accessible, performant, and visually refined. This role collaborates with designers, content strategists, demand generation, and our developer to elevate everything we release on absorblms.com http://absorblms.com, oversees implementation of components and pages in our Webflow-based stack, and shapes the architecture and reusable patterns for the team.

 

Unlike traditional IDE-only front-end roles, most production work is done in Webflow's visual development environment, requiring strong skills in HTML, CSS, DOM structure, responsive design, accessibility, browser performance, and client-side behavior. The ideal candidate is a browser-native web design engineer who cares about the quality of what ships.



What you’ll do:

 - Implement marketing website components, layouts, and pages to a high standard of HTML semantics, CSS quality, accessibility, performance, and visual polish.

 - Translate Figma designs into production web output, partnering with designers to refine responsive behavior, spacing, type, hierarchy, motion, and interaction details before and during implementation.

 - Own the quality bar for production web output, including reviewing work produced by other team members and helping establish standards, patterns, and reusable components.

 - Build custom interactive components – including Web Components and vanilla JavaScript modules – where the intersection of design craft and engineering is required.

 - Implement simple motion and animation as part of component and page work (e.g., CSS transitions, menu fade-ins, hover and focus states, lightweight interactions).

 - Contribute to our Next.js applications and shared component library (via Webflow DevLink) to keep design and behavior consistent across the marketing site and custom web apps.

 - Diagnose and resolve front-end issues using browser DevTools, including layout, rendering, performance, accessibility, and cross-browser problems.

 - Optimize Core Web Vitals and page load performance, including responsive image strategies, resource hints, render-blocking mitigation, script loading, and minimizing layout shift.

 - Implement accessibility best practices, including semantic HTML, heading structure, focus management, keyboard interaction, reduced-motion considerations, and appropriate ARIA usage.

 - Implement and QA A/B tests, personalization experiences, and conversion optimization experiments in partnership with marketing stakeholders.

 - Help reduce fragile one-off implementations by creating durable, reusable patterns and clear standards across components, layouts, and interactions.

 - Partner with the Content and Demand Gen teams as a technical resource for advanced layout, component, and interaction questions.

 - Contribute to the architecture and reusable patterns that underpin the marketing stack, including component design, CSS variable systems, and interaction patterns.

 - Make pragmatic trade-off decisions that balance design intent, engineering quality, timeline, and business impact.

What you’ll bring:

 - 8+ years of professional experience building production web interfaces at the intersection of design and front-end engineering.

 - Deep proficiency in semantic HTML and modern CSS, including grid, flexbox, custom properties, container queries, logical properties, and the full selector vocabulary.

 - Strong visual eye, with the ability to translate Figma designs faithfully and to identify a

source_jobs.title: Zuora System Specialist
source_jobs.description:
“What makes Absorb special is how genuinely we show up for each other. You’ll find smart, kind people who collaborate without ego, celebrate wins together, and step in quickly when someone needs support. That energy, paired with a shared drive to build something meaningful, is what makes this team such a great place to grow.” – Kim Jorgensen, Senior Manager – Billings and Accounts Payable Operations

At Absorb, we believe great systems create great experiences. As our Billing Systems Specialist, you'll play a key role in ensuring the technology behind our quote-to-cash process is accurate, scalable, and seamless. Sitting at the intersection of Salesforce, Zuora, Finance, and Sales, you'll own and optimize the systems that power our SaaS subscription business—from pricing and quoting to invoicing, renewals, and revenue operations. If you're energized by solving complex business challenges, improving processes through automation, and serving as the go-to expert for billing systems, this is an opportunity to make a meaningful impact in a high-growth, collaborative environment.




What you’ll do:

 - Own and optimize Zuora Billing, including product catalog management, pricing, subscriptions, invoicing, and payment processes.

 - Manage and troubleshoot the Salesforce–Zuora integration to ensure accurate data flow across the quote-to-cash lifecycle.

 - Improve billing operations through automation, process enhancements, and data quality initiatives that reduce manual effort.

 - Partner closely with Finance to support billing accuracy, revenue recognition, forecasting, and month-end activities.

 - Serve as the billing systems expert, resolving complex issues and collaborating with Sales, Customer Success, Support, and Finance teams.

 - Analyze billing data, monitor system performance, and identify trends, risks, and opportunities for operational improvement.

What you’ll bring:

 - Hands-on Zuora Billing experience — product catalog management, SKU configuration, workflow automation, subscription amendments, and reporting.

 - Understanding of the Salesforce ecosystem, ideally with exposure to the Zuora for Salesforce connector or CPQ.

 - Strong grasp of SaaS subscription billing concepts — ARR, MRR, proration, amendments, and co-terming.

 - Familiarity with quote-to-cash processes and where the handoffs between Sales, Finance, and Systems live.

 - Analytical mindset with a focus on continuous improvement and efficiency.

 - Comfort working across business and technical stakeholders — you can talk to a CFO and a Salesforce admin in the same afternoon.



Technologies we use:

 - Zuora

 - Salesforce

 - Jira

 - Freshservice

 - Copilot

 - Claude

 - ChatGPT

Are you ready to become an Absorber?

What we offer:

 - Fully remote-first work with flexible work arrangements 🏡

 - Comprehensive Health and Wellness Benefits including retirement savings programs, eligibility for two different bonus plans, generous time off, comprehensive medical and dental benefits based on your country of location 🧘

 - New Hire Equipment Allowance and monthly Flex Allowance to support your success 📦

 - Endless opportunity for career growth and internal mobility 🌱

 - Employee driven DE&I programs 🫶



Who are we?

Absorb Software is the leading global AI-driven learning platform provider, helping organizations unlock the full potential of their workforce. With its innovative Strategic Learning Systems (SLS) approach, Absorb empowers businesses to align learning with strategic goals, driving measurable impact and workforce agility. The Absorb LMS platform delivers personalized, scalable, and engaging learning experiences for employees, customers, and partners worldwide. Trusted by over 3,300 organizations and 34 million users, Absorb is redefining the future of workplace learning through cutting-edge AI, seamless integrations, and an unwavering commitment to innovation. We empower learners to enrich their lives, workplaces and communities.
